defaultComboState.description: Shadow Mark (5s): next skill hitting the marked target gets a per-skill bonus. Stab=guaranteed crit, Blade Dance=focused burst, FoK=+30% AoE, VS=+50% potency, Assassinate=-50% CD refund, CS=+2 chains, Blade Ward=double counter damage, Blade Trap=+50% trap damage, Shadow Dash=double mark.

mechanics: Mark Amplification (T1a — Shadow Mark's per-skill bonuses scale with consecutive marks on same target)
Hunter's Focus (T2 notable — marking a target below 50% HP triggers empowered mark with crit bonuses)
Death Warrant (T6 notable — consuming mark with Assassinate on low-HP target triggers execute bonus)
nonTracked: +X% damage on mark consume — T3 conditional

comboModifications.description: Shadow Mark's Assassinate bonus increased from -50% CD refund to -50% CD refund + guaranteed crit.

nodes.name: Repeated Marks
nodes.description: If Shadow Mark is cast on a target that already has an active Shadow Mark: the new mark's per-skill bonus is increased by +15% (multiplicative with the base bonus).
nodes.rank2: +25% per re-mark. Max 2 re-marks (+50% amplification).
nodes.designNotes: STACK_BUILDER on MARKS. Unique to setup skills — no other skill can 'double-mark' a target. Rewards spending 2-3 GCDs on marking before the payoff. Anti-efficiency tradeoff: more marks = stronger payoff but more time spent NOT dealing damage.

nodes.name: Opportunistic Mark
nodes.description: Shadow Mark's direct hit damage (50% WD) is increased by +15% for each active combo state on the player (Exposed, Deep Wound, etc.).
nodes.rank2: +20% per active combo state. If 3+ combo states active: Shadow Mark also applies Vulnerable (2s).
nodes.designNotes: Rewards having many combo states active when you mark. Different from every other T1 because this scales with the COMBO SYSTEM itself, not hits or targets. Setup skill that rewards other skills' setup.

nodes.name: Hunter's Focus
nodes.description: When Shadow Mark hits a target below 50% HP: the mark becomes a Hunter's Mark — all per-skill bonuses are doubled AND the marked target takes +15% damage from all sources. +5% crit chance.
nodes.designNotes: HP-gated mark upgrade. Different from Stab's Blade Sense (escalating crit proc) and Assassinate's Killing Intent (HP-gated guaranteed crit). SM's version upgrades the MARK ITSELF, not the skill that cast it. Low-HP targets get a stronger mark.

nodes.name: Mark Precision
nodes.description: While a Shadow Mark is active on any target: all your skills gain +5% crit chance.
nodes.rank2: +8% crit chance. Also +3% weapon mastery while any mark is active.
nodes.designNotes: Passive crit while mark is up. Since mark lasts 5s and SM has 6s CD, there's ~1s gap. Near-permanent uptime. Different from Assassinate's Executioner's Focus (crit while on CD) because this is tied to MARK PRESENCE, not skill CD.

nodes.name: Amplified Consume
nodes.description: +20% damage on the skill that consumes Shadow Mark.
nodes.rank2: +30% damage. Also +10% crit chance on the consuming skill.
nodes.designNotes: Flat consume bonus. Simple and powerful. Different from the per-skill bonuses (which are UNIQUE per consumer) — this is a flat bonus on TOP of the per-skill bonus.

nodes.name: Mark Duration
nodes.description: Shadow Mark duration increased by +2s (7s total).
nodes.rank2: +3s (8s total). If mark is consumed in the last 2s of its duration: +10% bonus damage on consume.
nodes.designNotes: Duration extension + late-consume bonus. Rank 2 rewards WAITING to consume — last-second consumption hits harder. Different from every other T3 because this modifies the MARK ITSELF, not the skill.

nodes.name: Setup Mastery
nodes.description: Shadow Mark's cooldown reduced by 1s (5s total).
nodes.rank2: CD reduced by 1.5s (4.5s total). +3% weapon mastery.
nodes.designNotes: CD reduction = more marks = more setup opportunities. Simple but powerful for a utility skill with 6s base CD.

nodes.name: Execute Setup
nodes.description: Shadow Mark's Assassinate bonus improved: instead of just -50% CD refund, Assassinate also gets guaranteed crit when consuming Shadow Mark. If target is below 30% HP: Assassinate deals +30% more damage on consume. +5% weapon mastery.
nodes.designNotes: Enhanced Assassinate-Mark synergy. Makes Shadow Mark → Assassinate the premium burst combo. The HP gate adds execute synergy.

nodes.name: Hunter's Patience
nodes.description: While Hunter's Mark (T2 upgraded mark) is active: +10% damage with all skills.
nodes.rank2: +15% damage. Hunter's Mark duration extended by 1s.
nodes.designNotes: Support for T2 Hunter's Mark state. Global damage during enhanced mark.

nodes.name: Perfect Setup
nodes.description: Shadow Mark's per-skill bonuses are ALWAYS at Hunter's Mark level (doubled) regardless of target HP. Mark duration +2s. +8% weapon mastery.
nodes.designNotes: Consistent choice. Every mark is an empowered mark. No HP gating. Reliable setup every cycle.

nodes.name: Double Mark
nodes.description: Shadow Mark can mark 2 targets simultaneously (hits primary + 1 adjacent). If both marks are consumed within 3s of each other: the second consume deals +60% damage. If only 1 is consumed: SM cooldown +2s.
nodes.designNotes: Risky double-mark. Two setups, two payoffs. If you consume both quickly → massive burst. If you only consume one → punished. Requires 2 separate consuming-skills hitting 2 different targets.

nodes.name: Death Warrant
nodes.description: When Assassinate consumes Shadow Mark on a target below 25% HP: trigger Death Warrant — Assassinate deals an additional hit for 200% of its fully-scaled damage. 15s ICD. +5% weapon mastery.
nodes.designNotes: The ultimate SM→Assassinate combo payoff. Setup (mark) + condition (low HP) + consume (Assassinate) = massive bonus hit. Three-layer gate makes it rare but devastating. Different from Assassinate's own Perfect Kill (consumes Exposed+Deep Wound) because this requires Shadow Mark specifically.

nodes.name: ARCHITECT OF DEATH
nodes.description: Shadow Mark deals 0 direct damage (was 50% WD). Mark per-skill bonuses tripled. Death Warrant (T6) triggers at 35% HP instead of 25% and bonus hit increased to 300%. SM cooldown increased by 2s (8s total). All non-SM skills deal -10% damage when no mark is active on any target.
nodes.tensionDescription: T6 is a conditional execute bonus on mark consume. T7 triples ALL mark bonuses and makes Death Warrant easier/stronger — but SM deals 0 damage itself, has 8s CD, and your OTHER skills are weaker without an active mark. You MUST mark constantly. The -10% penalty without mark means skipping SM in your rotation is a DPS loss.

mechanics: Plague Mark (T1a — marked targets take +X% ailment potency from all sources)
Festering Mark (T2 notable — consuming mark applies massive ailment at 3x potency)
Pandemic Mark (T6 notable — marked target death spreads mark to nearby + all ailments)
nonTracked: +X% ailment duration on marked — T3 conditional

comboModifications.description: Shadow Mark's per-skill bonuses now also apply +20% ailment potency on consume, regardless of which skill consumes.

nodes.name: Plague Mark
nodes.description: While Shadow Mark is active on a target: all ailments applied to that target gain +15% potency.
nodes.rank2: +20% potency. Also +8% ailment duration on marked targets.
nodes.designNotes: Mark as ailment AMPLIFIER. Completely different from Predator's mark stacking — this makes the mark a passive potency buff for ALL ailments on the target. SM becomes setup for DoT builds specifically.

nodes.name: Cursing Mark
nodes.description: Shadow Mark also applies Cursed (1 stack, 3s) to the target. Cursed reduces target resists by 15.
nodes.rank2: 2 stacks of Cursed (3s). Also applies Weakened (2s): target deals 10% less damage.
nodes.designNotes: Mark as CURSE applicator. SM becomes a debuff package: Shadow Mark + Cursed + Weakened. Different from any other T1 — no other skill applies curses as a baseline T1 behavior.

nodes.name: Festering Mark
nodes.description: When Shadow Mark is consumed by any skill: apply a bonus ailment at 3x the consuming skill's normal potency. The ailment type matches the consuming skill's element. +8% ailment duration.
nodes.designNotes: Consume → mega ailment. Different from VS's Venom Crit (crit → enhanced reapplication) because this triggers on MARK CONSUME, not crit. Any skill consuming the mark gets a bonus 3x ailment. Stab consume = 3x Stab ailment. Assassinate consume = 3x Assassinate ailment (huge snapshot).

nodes.name: Mark Amplifier
nodes.description: While a target has Shadow Mark: ailments tick 15% faster on that target.
nodes.rank2: 20% faster. Also +5% DoT multiplier on marked targets.
nodes.designNotes: Mark as tick speed buff. Different from FoK's Plague Wave (mass tick acceleration) because this is per-MARKED-TARGET, persistent while mark is active. REQUIRES ENGINE 10.5 (tick speed multiplier — per target, not global).

nodes.name: Lingering Curse
nodes.description: +15% ailment duration on marked targets.
nodes.rank2: +25% duration. Ailments applied during mark persist for 2s after mark expires.
nodes.designNotes: Duration bonus on marked targets. Simple conditional. Rank 2's post-mark persistence is unique.

nodes.name: Potent Consume
nodes.description: All mark consumptions also grant +20% ailment potency on the consuming skill, regardless of which skill consumes.
nodes.rank2: +30% potency. Also +10% DoT multiplier for 3s after consuming.
nodes.designNotes: COMBO ENHANCEMENT. Universal ailment potency on any mark consume. Makes SM a must-have for ailment builds regardless of which skill follows.

nodes.name: Mark Efficiency
nodes.description: Shadow Mark cooldown reduced by 0.5s for each ailment type active on the target when mark is applied.
nodes.rank2: 0.8s per ailment type (max -2.4s at 3 types). +3% weapon mastery.
nodes.designNotes: CD reduction from target's ailment state. More ailments on target = faster mark cycle. Rewards pre-ailmenting before marking. Unique mechanic.

nodes.name: Venom Setup
nodes.description: When Shadow Mark is consumed on a target with 5+ ailment stacks: enter Venom Setup state (4s). During this state: all ailment applications from any skill gain +25% potency AND have their duration refreshed to full. 12s ICD. +5% weapon mastery.
nodes.designNotes: State-shift from mark consume + ailment threshold. Different from Assassinate's Venom Executioner (8+ stacks on hit) because this triggers on MARK CONSUME, not direct hit. SM's setup role extends to creating burst windows for the whole rotation.

nodes.name: Setup Extension
nodes.description: While Venom Setup is active: +10% ailment duration for all skills.
nodes.rank2: +15% duration. Each ailment applied during window extends it by 0.3s (max +2s).
nodes.designNotes: Support for Venom Setup state.

nodes.name: Eternal Mark
nodes.description: Shadow Mark persists indefinitely until consumed (no duration limit). Marked targets take +10% ailment damage from all sources at all times. +8% weapon mastery.
nodes.designNotes: Consistent permanent mark. No rush to consume — mark stays until used. Constant +10% ailment damage on target. Patient, reliable.

nodes.name: Explosive Mark
nodes.description: When Shadow Mark expires WITHOUT being consumed: all ailments on the target burst for 50% of remaining damage as instant hit. If mark IS consumed normally: no burst, but consuming skill gets +40% potency.
nodes.designNotes: Risky choice: deliberately let marks expire for ailment burst, OR consume for potency. Two valid playstyles from one keystone. NOT consuming becomes a strategy.

nodes.name: Pandemic Mark
nodes.description: When a Shadow Marked target dies: Shadow Mark spreads to 2 nearby enemies (3s duration) AND all ailments from the dead target spread to marked targets at 75% potency. 10s ICD. +5% weapon mastery.
nodes.designNotes: Death-spread mark + ailment carry. Different from Stab's Pandemic Cascade (spread ailments only) because this also spreads the MARK ITSELF — creating new setup targets. Chain potential: kill marked target → mark spreads → kill new marked target → mark spreads again.

nodes.name: PLAGUE ARCHITECT
nodes.description: Shadow Mark can mark up to 3 targets simultaneously (hits primary + 2 adjacent). All marked targets take +20% ailment damage. Pandemic Mark (T6) triggers on ANY marked target death (no ICD). SM deals 0 direct damage. SM cooldown increased by 3s (9s total). Non-marked targets take -15% ailment damage from you.
nodes.tensionDescription: T6 spreads marks on death with ICD. T7 marks 3 targets simultaneously AND removes ICD on death-spread — but 0 direct damage, 9s CD, and non-marked targets take LESS ailment damage. You MUST mark targets for your ailments to work at full power. Skipping SM = -15% ailment damage everywhere.

mechanics: Defensive Mark (T1a — marked targets deal less damage to you specifically)
Shadow Warden (T2 notable — dodge while mark is active triggers counter + refreshes mark)
Phantom Mark (T6 notable — dodge triggers free SM counter that marks the attacker)

comboModifications.description: Shadow Mark also grants +10% dodge chance while any mark is active on an enemy.

nodes.name: Threat Mark
nodes.description: Shadow Marked targets deal 8% less damage to you.
nodes.rank2: 12% less damage. Also -5% attack speed for marked targets.
nodes.designNotes: Mark as DEFENSIVE debuff on target. Completely unique pattern — no other skill's T1a reduces incoming damage from a specific target. SM marks an enemy to make it LESS threatening, not just to set up damage.

nodes.name: Warding Presence
nodes.description: While any Shadow Mark is active on an enemy: +5% dodge chance.
nodes.rank2: +8% dodge chance. Also +5 all resist while mark is active.
nodes.designNotes: Passive defensive aura from mark presence. Different from other dodge nodes (per-hit, per-target) — this is simply 'mark exists = you're safer.' Nearly permanent with SM's CD.

nodes.name: Shadow Warden
nodes.description: When you dodge while Shadow Mark is active on any enemy: trigger a counter-attack on the marked target for 80% of SM's fully-scaled damage. Also refreshes Shadow Mark's duration. +1 Fortify stack. +5 all resist.
nodes.designNotes: Dodge → counter the MARKED target specifically (not the attacker). Also refreshes mark duration — creating near-permanent mark uptime through dodging. Different from Stab's Shadow Guard (counter the attacker) because this counters a SPECIFIC target you've chosen to mark.

nodes.name: Sentinel's Focus
nodes.description: While Shadow Mark is active: +10% damage reduction against the marked target's attacks.
nodes.rank2: +15% DR. Also +5% damage dealt to the marked target.
nodes.designNotes: Focused DR against the marked enemy. Different from Blade Dance's Scattered Threat (pack-size DR) and FoK's Suppressive Fire (all enemies deal less). This is targeted: one enemy is your focus, you're specifically defended against THEM.

nodes.name: Warden's Shield
nodes.description: +5% damage reduction while any Shadow Mark is active (flat DR from mark presence).
nodes.rank2: +8% DR. +3% weapon mastery.
nodes.designNotes: Flat DR from mark presence. Simple, nearly permanent defensive conditional.

nodes.name: Dodge Aura
nodes.description: Shadow Mark's consumption bonus now also grants +10% dodge chance for 4s (on top of per-skill bonus).
nodes.rank2: +15% dodge. Duration 5s.
nodes.designNotes: COMBO ENHANCEMENT. Mark consume → dodge window. Defensive payoff from consuming the mark, in addition to offensive per-skill bonus.

nodes.name: Mark Resilience
nodes.description: Each time you dodge while a mark is active: heal 1.5% max HP.
nodes.rank2: 2% max HP per dodge while marked. +3% weapon mastery.
nodes.designNotes: Dodge + mark = healing. Different from VS's ailment-tick healing and Stab's leech. This is dodge-frequency healing gated on MARK PRESENCE. More dodge = more heals, but only while you've marked something.

nodes.name: Shadow Sentinel
nodes.description: When you dodge 3 attacks while Shadow Mark is active on an enemy: enter Shadow Sentinel (4s). During this state: +20% DR, all counter-attacks (from any source) also hit the marked target. 10s ICD. +5% weapon mastery.
nodes.designNotes: State-shift from dodge-while-marked. Three dodges required — higher gate than Stab's Shadow Form (2 dodges). The 'counters also hit marked target' is unique — counter-attacks from Stab/BD/CS Ghost T2 nodes ALL target your marked enemy during this state. SM becomes the focus point for all defensive counter-attacks.

nodes.name: Sentinel Extension
nodes.description: While Shadow Sentinel is active: +10% dodge chance.
nodes.rank2: +15% dodge. Each dodge during Sentinel extends it by 0.4s (max +2s).
nodes.designNotes: Support for Shadow Sentinel. Dodge extension as usual.

nodes.name: Eternal Warden
nodes.description: Shadow Mark never expires while you have 2+ Fortify stacks. Marked targets deal 15% less damage to you. +8% weapon mastery.
nodes.designNotes: Consistent permanent mark with defensive scaling. Mark stays as long as you maintain Fortify. Reliable, always-on defense against marked target.

nodes.name: Counter Mark
nodes.description: Shadow Warden counter-attacks (T2) deal 150% instead of 80% and can crit. Each counter-crit reduces SM cooldown by 1s. You take +10% damage from non-marked enemies.
nodes.designNotes: Risky focused counter damage. Devastating counter-attacks against marked target, but vulnerable to everything else. Rewards single-target focus with SM mark.

nodes.name: Phantom Mark
nodes.description: When you dodge: 20% chance to trigger Phantom Mark — a free SM counter that marks the ATTACKER (not your current marked target) for 3s at 70% mark power. Does not replace existing mark. 8s ICD. +5% weapon mastery.
nodes.designNotes: Dodge → auto-mark the attacker. Creates temporary secondary marks on enemies that attack you. Different from all other T6 counters because this is a MARKING counter, not a damage counter. The mark itself is the reward — now two enemies are marked.

nodes.name: SHADOW WARDEN
nodes.description: Phantom Mark (T6) triggers on EVERY dodge (no chance, no ICD) at 40% mark power. All marked enemies deal 20% less damage to you. Shadow Mark deals 0 direct damage. SM cooldown increased by 2s (8s total). You take +15% damage from non-marked enemies.
nodes.tensionDescription: T6 is rare dodge → secondary mark. T7 fires EVERY dodge, constantly marking attackers. With multiple marked enemies dealing less damage, you're very safe against marked targets — but +15% damage from unmarked ones. Must mark threats proactively. SM deals 0 damage, long CD — pure utility/defense.
